repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
names_ssim: Dionysius, the Areopagite, Saint, active 1st century
titles_tesim: Index and summary of the fifteen chapters, ff. 79r-80v
Pseudo-Dionysius' De divinis nominibus, ch. four (excerpt)
names_tesim: Dionysius, the Areopagite, Saint, active 1st century
exerpts_tesim: By Dionysius the Areopagite, bishop of Athens, to Timothy the bishop on the leadership of the heavenly priesthood…
Come, Lord Jesus, yes, Jesus Christ, with all the saints, possessors, the scribe, and the hearer. Amen
It is possible that one might say that the beautiful good is longed for, preferred, and loved…
Preface of the treatise by our righteous father, Maximus the Confessor, on the interpretation of the visible deeds in the holy church on the sacramental side
The revelation of Jesus Christ which God gave him to show his servants what would happen soon…

With Christ, our great God alone, our Savior and Deliverer to Whom is the glory, honor, esteem, dignity, and majesty with the Father and Holy Spirit to the end of the ages. And glory to God forever and ever
ات يا رب يسوع نعم الرب يسوع المسيح مع جميع القديسين والمقتنى والناسح والسامع امين
جليان يسوع المسيح الدي اعطاه الله ان يري عبيده ما ينبغي ان يكون سريعا
عند المسيح الاهنا العظيم وحده مخلصنا ومنقد الدي له المجد والعز والاكرام والجلال والاعظام الا مع ابيه والروح القدس الى اباد الدهور والسبح لله دايما ابدا
قد يجوز ان يقول الانسان ان الخيّر الصالح اذا كان معشوقاً ماثوراً محبوباً
لابينا البار مكسمس المعترف مقدمه كلامه في تفسيره الافعال المنظورة اليها في الكنيسه المتقدسه على جهه سريه
لذيونيسَيوس الاروباجيتس اسقف ايثنا الي تيموثاوسَ الاسقف عن رىاسَه الكهنوت السَماييه
وانه ماثور معشوق محبوب وغير ذلك مما أوضحه فيما تقدم القول الصادق  ولربنا التسبحة الي دهد الادهار امين
وكان غير خاير ولا عاذل عن تركيبه والسبح لله دائما ابدا
…and he has not departed nor is he excused from this composition. Praise to God forever and always
…and that which is longed for, preferred, and loved and the other things that were explained previously in this genuine treatise, and to our Lord be the glory to the age of ages. Amen
contents_tesim: Bible. Revelation
By Dionysius the Areopagite, bishop of Athens, to Timothy the bishop on the leadership of the heavenly priesthood…
Celestial hierarchy
Chapter Eight, ff. 109v-114r
Chapter Eleven, ff. 122v-124r
Chapter Five, ff. 99v-101r
Chapter Four, ff. 95r-99v
Chapter Fourteen, ff. 133r-143v
Chapter Nine, ff. 114r-121r
Chapter One, ff. 80v-83r
Chapter Seven, ff. 102v-109v
Chapter Six, ff. 101r-102v
Chapter Ten, ff. 121r-122v
Chapter Thirteen, ff. 132r-133r
Chapter Three, ff. 92r-95r
Chapter Twelve, ff. 124r-132r
Chapter Two, ff. 83r-92r
Come, Lord Jesus, yes, Jesus Christ, with all the saints, possessors, the scribe, and the hearer. Amen
De coelesti hierarchia
Index and summary of the fifteen chapters, ff. 79r-80v
It is possible that one might say that the beautiful good is longed for, preferred, and loved…
Mystagogia (Maximus the Confessor)
On Divine Names (Dionysius)
Preface of the treatise by our righteous father, Maximus the Confessor, on the interpretation of the visible deeds in the holy church on the sacramental side
Pseudo-Dionysius' De divinis nominibus, ch. four (excerpt)
The revelation of Jesus Christ which God gave him to show his servants what would happen soon…

There are section divisions within the text, but these appear to have been added after the text was written. The sections are numbered from three to seven (e.g. ff. 3r-8v), after which, there are blank portions on later folia, perhaps where another scribe was meant to add in the section numbers.
There are several sections throughout, some with rubrics and others without. See, for instance, f. 158r (without rubric), 165v (with rubric), etc.
This text contains rubrics.  A rubric for Chapter Fifteen is lacking.
This text is an excerpt from Pseudo-Dionysius' De divinis nominibus, chapter four (CPG 6602)
Varia

With Christ, our great God alone, our Savior and Deliverer to Whom is the glory, honor, esteem, dignity, and majesty with the Father and Holy Spirit to the end of the ages. And glory to God forever and ever
